skill.name: css-animations
skill.description: CSS animation adapter patterns for HyperFrames. Use when authoring CSS keyframes, animation-delay based timing, animation-fill-mode, animation-play-state, or CSS-only motion that HyperFrames must seek deterministically during preview and rendering.
skill.summary: Write CSS keyframe animations that work with deterministic frame seeking in HyperFrames video compositions.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: All 26 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a single markdown documentation file (SKILL.md, 125 lines) with no executable code. The 19 external_commands flags detected markdown code fence backticks as Ruby shell execution, which is incorrect. The 2 network flags are legitimate MDN CSS documentation reference URLs. The high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm finding at line 3 is a scanner error reading YAML frontmatter description text. The 4 system reconnaissance flags are CSS property values and documentation advice. No prompt injection attempts were detected. This skill is safe to publish.

content.user_title: Create CSS Animations for HyperFrames Video Compositions
content.value_statement: HyperFrames compositions need deterministic frame seeking that standard CSS animations do not provide. This skill teaches patterns for writing CSS keyframe animations that work correctly with the HyperFrames preview and rendering pipeline.
actual_capabilities: Generate CSS keyframe animations compatible with HyperFrames deterministic frame seeking
Apply data-start and data-duration attributes for timeline synchronization
Create stagger animation patterns using CSS custom properties
Configure finite animation-iteration-count and animation-fill-mode for predictable rendering
Recommend transform-based animation over layout property animation for performance
Apply mask, glow, shimmer, and parallax effects as CSS-only motion layers
limitations: Does not support infinite CSS animations in environments without WAAPI-backed seekable handles
Cannot choreograph complex multi-element timelines; use GSAP skill for that
Does not respond to user interaction events like hover, focus, or scroll
Only works within HyperFrames compositions, not as standalone CSS

use_cases.title: Add decorative motion to video backgrounds
use_cases.description: Motion designers creating HyperFrames video compositions can use CSS keyframe animations for background ambient motion, particle-like effects, and decorative loops without writing full JavaScript timelines.

use_cases.title: Animate element entrances in video clips
use_cases.description: Video editors need simple single-element entrance animations like fades, slides, and scale-ins for text overlays and graphics. This skill provides CSS-only patterns within the HyperFrames rendering pipeline.

use_cases.title: Create shimmer and glow effects for branding
use_cases.description: Content producers adding polish to video compositions can apply shimmer, glow, and mask animations to logos and titles using CSS animations compatible with deterministic frame seeking.

prompt_templates.title: Simple pulse ring animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CSS pulse-ring animation for a circular element. Use 1200ms duration, 3 iterations, fill-mode both, and a cubic-bezier timing function. Include a data-start attribute and link the animation to the clip timeline.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a pulsing ring effect to a composition element

prompt_templates.title: Staggered dot sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a CSS stagger animation for a group of dots. Use CSS custom properties for delay calculation. Each dot should pop in with a scale and opacity transition offset by 120ms intervals.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating staggered entrance animation for multiple elements

prompt_templates.title: Shimmer overlay for text
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a shimmer animation using a CSS gradient that translates horizontally across an element. Use background-position animation with a gradient mask. Set finite iteration count matching the clip duration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding shimmer or gloss motion to text or surfaces

prompt_templates.title: Mask reveal animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CSS mask reveal animation that uncovers content from left to right. Use a clip-path inset animation or mask-image with a gradient. Ensure the animation has finite iteration and proper fill-mode for seekable preview.
prompt_templates.scenario: Revealing content with a clip-path or mask animation

output_examples.input: Add a pulsing ring to my composition element
output_examples.output: Creates a CSS keyframe animation named pulse-ring that scales a circular element from 0.82 to 1.18 while fading opacity from 0 to 1 and back. The animation runs 3 times over 1200ms each with a smooth cubic-bezier curve. HTML markup includes data-start and data-duration attributes for HyperFrames timeline sync.

output_examples.input: Create staggered dot entrance for three dots
output_examples.output: Generates three span elements inside a container, each with a CSS custom property --i set to 0, 1, and 2. A shared dot-pop keyframe animation bounces each dot up from below with a scale-in effect. Animation-delay is calculated from the custom property for the stagger effect.

output_examples.input: Build a shimmer overlay for heading text
output_examples.output: Creates an overlay with a diagonal gradient that slides horizontally across the text. Uses background-position animation with background-size to create a metallic shine effect. The animation has finite iterations matching the composition duration.

best_practices: Use finite animation-iteration-count that covers the visible duration instead of infinite loops
Set animation-fill-mode to both so the animated element holds its state before and after active motion
Prefer animating transform and opacity over layout properties like top, left, width, or height
anti_patterns: Using infinite animations without verifying the browser exposes seekable WAAPI-backed CSS animation handles
Animating layout properties like top, left, width, or height when CSS transforms can achieve the same effect
Relying on hover, focus, scroll, or media query states to trigger render-critical motion

faq.question: Can I use infinite CSS animations?
faq.answer: Only if you have verified that the browser exposes seekable WAAPI-backed CSS animation handles. Prefer a finite iteration count covering the visible duration for reliable seeking.

faq.question: How does HyperFrames seek CSS animations?
faq.answer: The adapter discovers elements with computed animation-name, seeks their browser Animation handles when available, and falls back to pausing with negative animation-delay.

faq.question: What is the data-start attribute for?
faq.answer: It sets the local animation time to match the clip timeline. This ensures the animation starts at the correct point when seeking during preview and rendering.

faq.question: Why should I use finite iteration count?
faq.answer: The negative-delay fallback cannot represent unbounded duration in environments without WAAPI-backed CSS animations. Finite count ensures predictable seeking behavior.

faq.question: Can I combine CSS animations with GSAP?
faq.answer: Yes. CSS animations work best for simple one-element motion with fixed duration. Use GSAP for complex multi-element timeline choreography within the same composition.

faq.question: What happens if my animation uses layout properties?
faq.answer: Layout properties like top and left cause browser reflow and degrade render performance. The skill recommends using CSS transforms for the same visual effect.
